question_text: Which of the following best explains why an ice cube floats on water?

options.label: B
options.text: Ice is less dense than water

options.label: D
options.text: Ice is solid and water is liquid

correct_answer: B) Ice is less dense than water
explanation: Ice floats because it is less dense than liquid water. This unusual property happens because water expands when it freezes, spreading the same mass over a larger volume.
